You are polite and patient, and prefer to assume good intent, trying your best to be helpful. If you are unsure or have assumptions, you prefer to test them yourself or ask questions to clarify requirements.

General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.

   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-assistant/hive-mind --branch issue-1043-824a8917a5fe --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-assistant/hive-mind --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an examples and/or experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, use WebFetch tool (or fetch tool) to download the image first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's HTML 404 pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and may crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\" or \"text\", the download failed and you should retry or skip the image.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-assistant [keywords].

   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1044/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1044/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1044/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!

   - When you finalize the pull request:

   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.

It provides programmatic control and command-line interfaces for orchestrating agent processes.\n\n## Core Purpose\n\nThe library abstracts interactions with various AI agent CLIs—including Anthropic's Claude Code CLI, OpenAI's Codex, OpenCode, and @link-assistant/agent—into a unified interface. As stated in the documentation, it's \"built on the success of hive-mind\" and provides \"flexible JavaScript interface and CLI tools for managing agent processes.\"\n\n## Key Features\n\n- **Multi-tool support**: Works with claude, codex, opencode, and agent CLIs\n- **Isolation modes**: Direct execution, GNU Screen sessions, or Docker containers\n- **JSON streaming**: NDJSON input/output for real-time message processing\n- **Model mapping**: Automatic alias-to-full-ID conversion (e.g., \"sonnet\" → \"claude-sonnet-4-5-...\")\n- **Execution modes**: Detached background tasks or attached real-time monitoring\n- **Dry-run capability**: Preview commands before execution\n\n## Primary CLI Commands\n\n**start-agent** initiates an agent with configuration options:\n```\n--tool, --working-directory, --prompt, --model, --isolation, --detached, --dry-run\n```\n\n**stop-agent** terminates detached processes running in screen or Docker environments.\n\n## JavaScript API Usage\n\nThe library exports an `agent()` function accepting configuration objects, with `start()` and `stop()` methods returning execution results including exit codes, plain/parsed output, session IDs, and token usage metrics."

${tempDir}`);\n    }\n  } else {\n    tempDir = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `gh-issue-solver-${Date.now()}`);\n    await fs.mkdir(tempDir, { recursive: true });\n    await log(`\\nCreating temporary directory: ${tempDir}`);\n  }\n\n  return { tempDir, isResuming };\n};\n\n// Handle fork creation and repository setup\nexport const setupRepository = async (argv, owner, repo) => {\n  let repoToClone = `${owner}/${repo}`;\n  let forkedRepo = null;\n  let upstreamRemote = null;\n\n  if (argv.fork) {\n    await log(`\\n${formatAligned('🍴', 'Fork mode:', 'ENABLED')}`);\n    await log(`${formatAligned('', 'Checking fork status...', '')}\\n`);\n\n    // Get current user\n    const userResult = await $`gh api user --jq .login`;\n    if (userResult.code !== 0) {\n      await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'Error:', 'Failed to get current user')}`);\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    const currentUser = userResult.stdout.toString().trim();\n\n    // Determine fork name based on --prefix-fork-name-with-owner-name option\n    const forkRepoName = argv.prefixForkNameWithOwnerName ? `${owner}-${repo}` : repo;\n    const forkFullName = `${currentUser}/${forkRepoName}`;\n\n    // Check if fork already exists\n    const forkCheckResult = await $`gh repo view ${forkFullName} --json name 2>/dev/null`;\n\n    if (forkCheckResult.code === 0) {\n      // Fork exists\n      await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Fork exists:', forkFullName)}`);\n      repoToClone = forkFullName;\n      forkedRepo = forkFullName;\n      upstreamRemote = `${owner}/${repo}`;\n    } else {\n      // Need to create fork\n      await log(`${formatAligned('🔄', 'Creating fork...', '')}`);\n      let forkResult;\n      if (argv.prefixForkNameWithOwnerName) {\n        // Use --fork-name flag to create fork with owner prefix\n        forkResult = await $`gh repo fork ${owner}/${repo} --fork-name ${forkRepoName} --clone=false`;\n      } else {\n        // Standard fork creation (no custom name)\n        forkResult = await $`gh repo fork ${owner}/${repo} --clone=false`;\n      }\n\n      // Check if fork creation failed or if fork already exists\n      if (forkResult.code !== 0) {\n        await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'Error:', 'Failed to create fork')}`);\n        await log(forkResult.stderr ? forkResult.stderr.toString() : 'Unknown error');\n        process.exit(1);\n      }\n\n      // Check if the output indicates the fork already exists (from parallel worker)\n      const forkOutput = forkResult.stderr ? forkResult.stderr.toString() : '';\n      if (forkOutput.includes('already exists')) {\n        // Fork was created by another worker - treat as if fork already existed\n        await log(`${formatAligned('ℹ️', 'Fork exists:', 'Already created by another worker')}`);\n        await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Using existing fork:', forkFullName)}`);\n\n        // Retry verification with exponential backoff\n        // GitHub may need time to propagate the fork visibility across their infrastructure\n        const maxRetries = 5;\n        const baseDelay = 2000; // Start with 2 seconds\n        let forkVerified = false;\n\n        for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {\n          const delay = baseDelay * Math.pow(2, attempt - 1); // 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s\n          await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Verifying fork:', `Attempt ${attempt}/${maxRetries} (waiting ${delay / 1000}s)...`)}`);\n          await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));\n\n          const reCheckResult = await $`gh repo view ${forkFullName} --json name 2>/dev/null`;\n          if (reCheckResult.code === 0) {\n            forkVerified = true;\n            await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Fork verified:', 'Successfully confirmed fork exists')}`);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n\n        if (!forkVerified) {\n          await log(`${formatAligned('❌', 'Error:', 'Fork reported as existing but not found after multiple retries')}`);\n          await log(`${formatAligned('', 'Suggestion:', 'GitHub may be experiencing delays - try running the command again in a few minutes')}`);\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n      } else {\n        await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Fork created:', forkFullName)}`);\n\n        // Wait a moment for fork to be ready\n        await log(`${formatAligned('⏳', 'Waiting:', 'For fork to be ready...')}`);\n        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 3000));\n      }\n\n      repoToClone = forkFullName;\n      forkedRepo = forkFullName;\n      upstreamRemote = `${owner}/${repo}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { repoToClone, forkedRepo, upstreamRemote };\n};\n\n// Error handling with log attachment\nexport const handl**********Error = async (error, shouldAttachLogs, owner, repo, argv = {}) => {\n  await log('Error executing command:', cleanErrorMessage(error));\n  await log(`Stack trace: ${error.stack}`, { verbose: true });\n\n  // If --attach-logs is enabled, try to attach failure logs\n  if (shouldAttachLogs && getLogFile()) {\n    await log('\\n📄 Attempting to attach failure logs...');\n\n    // Try to attach to existing PR first\n    if (global.createdPR && global.createdPR.number) {\n      try {\n        const logUploadSuccess = await attachLogToGitHub({\n          logFile: getLogFile(),\n          targetType: 'pr',\n          targetNumber: global.createdPR.number,\n          owner,\n          repo,\n          $,\n          log,\n          sanitizeLogContent,\n          verbose: argv.verbose || false,\n          errorMessage: cleanErrorMessage(error),\n        });\n\n        if (logUploadSuccess) {\n          await log('📎 Failure log attached to Pull Request');",

`validateClaudeConnection(model)`\n  - `executeClaude(params)`\n  - `executeClaudeCommand(params)`\n  \n- **`/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/codex.lib.mjs`** (520 lines)\n  - `validateCodexConnection(model)`\n  - `executeCodex(params)`\n  - `executeCodexCommand(params)`\n  \n- **`/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/opencode.lib.mjs`** (541 lines)\n  - `validateOpenCodeConnection(model)`\n  - `executeOpenCode(params)`\n  - `executeOpenCodeCommand(params)`\n  \n- **`/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/agent.lib.mjs`** (684 lines)\n  - `validateAgentConnection(model)`\n  - `executeAgent(params)`\n  - `executeAgentCommand(params)`\n  - `parseAgentTokenUsage(output)`\n\n### 6. **Tool Connection Validation**\n\n**Location:** `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/solve.mjs` line 217\n\nTools are validated via `performSystemChecks()` from `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/solve.validation.lib.mjs`:\n- Validates model names (always runs)\n- Validates tool connection (skipped with `--skip-tool-connection-check`)\n\nEach tool has its own validation function:\n- `validateClaudeConnection(model)` \n- `validateCodexConnection(model)`\n- `validateOpenCodeConnection(model)`\n- `validateAgentConnection(model)` [need to verify]\n\n### 7. **Model Validation System**\n\n**Location:** `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/model-validation.lib.mjs`\n\nDefines model mappings for each tool:\n- `CLAUDE_MODELS` - Supported Claude models (sonnet, opus, haiku, haiku-3-5, haiku-3)\n- `OPENCODE_MODELS` - Supported OpenCode models (grok, grok-code, gpt4, claude, etc.)\n- `CODEX_MODELS` - Supported Codex models (gpt5, o3, gpt4, claude, etc.)\n- `AGENT_MODELS` - Supported Agent models (grok, big-pickle, sonnet, opus, etc.)\n\nFunctions:\n- `getModelMapForTool(tool)` - Returns model map for tool\n- `validateAndExitOnInvalidModel(model, tool, safeExit)` - Validates model names\n\n### 8. **Key Integration Points for `--use-agent-commander`**\n\nBased on the code structure, adding the `--use-agent-commander` option would require changes in:\n\n1. **`/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/solve.config.lib.mjs`**\n   - Add new option `.option('use-agent-commander', {...})`\n   - Mark as experimental (hidden: true)\n\n2. **`/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/solve.mjs`**\n   - Line 115-127: Conditionally import agent-commander library instead of tool-specific ones\n   - Line 210-211: Tool validation logic (may need agent-commander validation)\n   - Line 743-869: Tool execution logic - add handling for agent-commander mode\n   - Line 116-127: Update check****************anges to use agent-commander if enabled\n\n3. **New File: `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/agent-commander.lib.mjs`** (or import from external package)\n   - Needs: `validateAgentCommanderConnection(model, tool)`\n   - Needs: `executeAgentCommander(params)` - Unified executor for all tools\n   - Needs: `executeAgentCommanderCommand(params)`\n   - Needs: Tool/model routing logic\n\n4. **Hive Mode (`/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/hive.config.lib.mjs` & `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767183061084/src/hive.mjs`)**\n   - May need similar option support if hive also supports agent selection\n   - Currently hive supports `--tool` option (line 112-117 of hive.config.lib.mjs)\n\n### 9. **Configuration Pattern Comparison**\n\n**Current Tool Selection:**\n```bash\nsolve.mjs <issue-url> --tool claude|codex|opencode|agent --model <model-name>\n```\n\n**Proposed Agent-Commander Integration:**\n```bash\nsolve.mjs <issue-url> --use-agent-commander --tool claude|codex|opencode|agent --model <model-name>\n```\n\nThe `--use-agent-commander` flag would act as a **mode switch** to delegate all tool handling to the agent-commander repository while keeping the same command-line interface.\n\n### 10. **File Organization Summary**\n\n**Key Files to Modify:**\n| File | Purpose | Lines |\n|------|---------|-------|\n| `/src/solve.config.lib.mjs` | Add CLI option | 299 lines |\n| `/src/solve.mjs` | Main execution logic | 1267 lines |\n| `/src/model-validation.lib.mjs` | Model validation | 210+ lines |\n| `/src/solve.validation.lib.mjs` | System checks | ~100 lines |\n\n**Related but Unchanged:**\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `/src/hive.mjs` | Hive monitoring (needs separate assessment) |\n| `/src/hive.config.lib.mjs` | Hive CLI config (needs separate assessment) |\n| `/src/claude.lib.mjs`, `/src/codex.lib.mjs`, `/src/opencode.lib.mjs`, `/src/agent.lib.mjs` | Tool executors (can coexist with agent-commander) |\n\n### 11. **Implementation Recommendations**\n\n1. **Add flag as experimental** - Use `hidden: true` in options (following existing pattern for experimental features)\n2. **Default disabled** - Set `default: false` for backward compatibility\n3. **Graceful fallback** - If agent-commander is not available/installed, fall back to existing tools\n4. **Consistent interface** - Ensure agent-commander respects same params as current execute functions\n5. **Documentation** - Update help text to note that this feature delegates to separate agent-commander repo\n6. **Error messaging** - capture output\n   316→    process.stderr.write = function (chunk, encoding, callback) {\n   317→      stderrBuffer.push(chunk.toString());\n   318→      // Call the callback if provided (for compatibility)\n   319→      if (typeof encoding === 'function') {\n   320→        encoding();\n   321→      } else if (typeof callback === 'function') {\n   322→        callback();\n   323→      }\n stderr.write\n   331→      process.stderr.write = originalStderrWrite;\n   332→\n   333→      // In verbose mode, show what was captured from stderr (for debugging)\n   334→      if (global.verboseMode && stderrBuffer.length > 0) {\n   335→        const captured = stderrBuffer.join('');\n   336→        if (captured.trim()) {\n   337→          console.error('[Suppressed yargs stderr]:', captured);\n   338→        }\n   339→      }\n   340→    }\n   341→  } catch (error) {\n   342→    // Yargs throws errors for validation issues\n   343→    // If the error is about unknown arguments (strict mode), re-throw it\n   344→    if (error.message && error.message.includes('Unknown arguments')) {\n   345→      throw error;\n   346→    }\n   347→    // For other validation errors, show a warning in verbose mode\n   348→    if (error.message && global.verboseMode) {\n   349→      console.error('Yargs parsing warning:', error.message);\n   350→    }\n   351→    // Try to get the argv even with the error\n   352→    argv = error.argv || {};\n   353→  }\n   354→\n   355→  // Post-processing: Fix model default for opencode and codex tools\n   356→  // Yargs doesn't properly handle dynamic defaults based on other arguments,\n   357→  // so we need to handle this manually after parsing\n   358→  const model*************vided = rawArgs.includes('--model') || rawArgs.includes('-m');\n   359→\n   360→  // Normalize alias flags: legacy --skip-tool-check and --skip-claude-check behave like --skip-tool-connection-check\n   361→  if (argv) {\n   362→    //  375→  } else if (argv.tool === 'codex' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n   376→    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for codex\n   377→    argv.model = 'gpt-5';\n   378→  } else if (argv.tool === 'agent' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n   379→    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for agent\n   380→    argv.model = 'grok-code';\n   381→  }\n   382→\n   383→  // Validate mutual exclusivity of --claude-file and --gitkeep-file\n   384→  // Check if both are explicitly enabled (user passed both --claude-file and --gitkeep-file)\n   385→  if (argv.claudeFile && argv.gitkeepFile) {\n   386→    // Check if they were explicitly set via command line\n   387→    const claudeFileExplicit = new Error('--claude-file and --gitkeep-file are mutually exclusive. Ple
[2025-12-31T12:13:38.017Z] [INFO] ase use only one.');\n   392→    }\n   393→\n   394→    // If only one is explicit, turn off the other\n   395→    if (gitkeepFileExplicit && !claudeFileExplicit) {\n   396→      argv.claudeFile = false;\n   397→    } else if (claudeFileExplicit && !gitkeepFileExplicit) {\n   398→      argv.gitkeepFile = false;\n   399→    }\n   400→  }\n   401→\n   402→  // Check for both being disabled (both --no-claude-file and --no-gitkeep-file)\n   403→  const noClaudeFile = rawArgs.includes('--no-claude-file');\n   404→  const noGitkeepFile = rawArgs.includes('--no-gitkeep-file');\n   405→\n   406→  if (noClaudeFile && noGitkeepFile) {\n   407→    throw new Error('Cannot disable both --claude-file and --gitkeep-file. command line arguments - now needs yargs and hideBin passed in\nexport const parseArguments = async (yargs, hideBin) => {\n  const rawArgs = hideBin(process.argv);\n  // Use .parse() instead of .argv to ensure .strict() mode works correctly\n  // When you call yargs(args) and use .argv, strict mode doesn't trigger\n  // See: https://github.com/yargs/yargs/issues - .strict() only works with .parse()\n\n  let argv;\n  try {\n    // Suppress stderr output from yargs during parsing to prevent validation errors from appearing\n    // This prevents \"YError: Not enough arguments\" from polluting stderr (issue #583)\n    // Save the original stderr.write\n    const originalStderrWrite = process.stderr.write;\n    const stderrBuffer = [];\n\n    // Temporarily override stderr.write to capture output\n    process.stderr.write = function (chunk, encoding, callback) {\n      stderrBuffer.push(chunk.toString());\n      // Call the callback if provided (for compatibility)\n      if (typeof encoding === 'function') {\n        encoding();\n      } else if (typeof callback === 'function') {\n        callback();\n      }\n      return true;\n    };\n\n    try {\n      argv = await createYargsConfig(yargs()).parse(rawArgs);\n    } finally {\n      // Always restore stderr.write\n      process.stderr.write = originalStderrWrite;\n\n      // In verbose mode, show what was captured from stderr (for debugging)\n      if (global.verboseMode && stderrBuffer.length > 0) {\n        const captured = stderrBuffer.join('');\n        if (captured.trim()) {\n          console.error('[Suppressed yargs stderr]:', captured);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    // Yargs throws errors for validation issues\n    // If the error is about unknown arguments (strict mode), re-throw it\n    if (error.message && error.message.includes('Unknown arguments')) {\n      throw error;\n    }\n    // For other validation errors, show a warning in verbose mode\n    if (error.message && global.verboseMode) {\n      console.error('Yargs parsing warning:', error.message);\n    }\n    // Try to get the argv even with the error\n    argv = error.argv || {};\n  }\n\n parsing\n  const model*************vided = rawArgs.includes('--model') || rawArgs.includes('-m');\n\n  // Normalize alias flags: legacy --skip-tool-check and --skip-claude-check behave like --skip-tool-connection-check\n  if (argv) {\n    // Support deprecated flags\n    if (argv.skipToolCheck || argv.skipClaudeCheck) {\n      argv.skipToolConnectionCheck = true;\n    }\n    // Support negated deprecated flag: --no-tool-check becomes --no-tool-connection-check\n    if (argv.toolCheck === false) {\n      argv.toolConnectionCheck = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (argv.tool === 'opencode' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for opencode\n    argv.model = 'grok-code-fast-1';\n  } else if (argv.tool === 'codex' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for codex\n    argv.model = 'gpt-5';\n  } else if (argv.tool === 'agent' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for agent\n    argv.model = 'grok-code';\n  }\n\n  // Validate mutual exclusivity of explicitly set via command line\n    const claudeFileExplicit = rawArgs.includes('--claude-file');\n    const gitkeepFileExplicit = rawArgs.includes('--gitkeep-file');\n\n    if (claudeFileExplicit && gitkeepFileExplicit) {\n      throw new Error('--claude-file and --gitkeep-file are mutually exclusive. Please use only one.');\n    }\n\n    // If only one is explicit, turn off the other\n    if (gitkeepFileExplicit && !claudeFileExplicit) {\n      argv.claudeFile = false;\n    } else if (claudeFileExplicit && !gitkeepFileExplicit) {\n      argv.gitkeepFile = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check for both being disabled (both --no-claude-file and --no-gitkeep-file)\n  const noClaudeFile = rawArgs.includes('--no-claude-file');\n  const noGitkeepFile = rawArgs.includes('--no-gitkeep-file');\n\n  if (noClaudeFile && noGitkeepFile) {\n    throw new Error('Cannot disable both --claude-file and --gitkeep-file. At least one must be enabled for PR creation.');\n  }\n\n  // If user explicitly set --no-claude-file, enable gitkeep-file\n  if (noClaudeFile && !argv.gitkeepFile) {\n    argv.gitkeepFile = true;\n    argv.claudeFile = false;\n  }\n\n  // If user explicitly set --no-gitkeep-file, enable claude-file (this is the default anyway)\n  if (noGitkeepFile && !argv.claudeFile) {\n    argv.claudeFile = true;\n    argv.gitkeepFile = false;\n  }\n\n  return argv;\n};\n","numLines":424,"startLine":1,"totalLines":424}}}

[2025-12-31T12:14:48.157Z] [INFO] {
  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
      {
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_01BHxKmkmrwGb3DkF7eVSJcg",
        "type": "tool_result",
        "content": "/**\n * Agent Commander - Main library interface\n * A JavaScript library to control agents enclosed in CLI commands\n *\n * Supports multiple CLI agents:\n * - claude: Anthropic Claude Code CLI\n * - codex: OpenAI Codex CLI\n * - opencode: OpenCode CLI\n * - agent: @link-assistant/agent (unrestricted OpenCode fork)\n */\n\nimport {\n  buildAgentCommand,\n  buildScreenStopCommand,\n  buildDockerStopCommand,\n} from './command-builder.mjs';\nimport {\n  executeCommand,\n  executeDetached,\n  setupSignalHandler,\n  startCommand,\n} from './executor.mjs';\nimport { getTool, isToolSupported } from './tools/index.mjs';\nimport { createOutputStream, createInputStream } from './streaming/index.mjs';\n\n/**\n * Parse JSON messages from output if the tool supports it\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.output - Raw output to parse\n * @param {string} [options.toolName] - Tool name for tool-specific parsing\n * @returns {Array|null} Array of parsed JSON messages or null if parsing fails\n */\nfunction parseJsonMessages(options) {\n  const { output, toolName } = options;\n\n  // If we have a tool-specific parser, use it\n  if (toolName && isToolSupported({ toolName })) {\n    const tool = getTool({ toolName });\n    if (tool.parseOutput) {\n      return tool.parseOutput({ output });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Default parsing logic\n  try {\n    // Try to parse as JSON array\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(output);\n    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {\n      return parsed;\n    }\n    // If single object, wrap in array\n    return [parsed];\n  } catch {\n    // Try to extract JSON objects line by line (NDJSON)\n    const lines = output.split('\\n');\n    const messages = [];\n    for (const line of lines) {\n      const trimmed = line.trim();\n      if (trimmed.startsWith('{') || trimmed.startsWith('[')) {\n        try {\n          const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);\n          messages.push(parsed);\n        } catch {\n          // Skip lines that aren't valid JSON\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    return messages.length > 0 ? messages : null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Create an agent controller\n * @param {Object} options - Agent configuration\n * @param {string} options.tool - CLI tool to use (e.g., 'claude', 'codex', 'opencode', 'agent')\n * @param {string} options.workingDirectory - Working directory for the agent\n * @param {string} [options.prompt] - Prompt for the agent\n * @param {string} [options.systemPrompt] - System prompt for the agent\n * @param {string} [options.model] - Model to use (tool-specific)\n * @param {string} [options.isolation='none'] - Isolation mode: 'none', 'screen', 'docker'\n * @param {string} [options.screenName] - Screen session name (for screen isolation)\n * @param {string} [options.containerName] - Container name (for docker isolation)\n * @param {boolean} [options.json=false] - Enable JSON output mode\n * @param {string} [options.resume] - Resume a previous session (tool-specific)\n * @param {Object} [options.toolOptions] - Additional tool-specific options\n * @returns {Object} Agent controller with start, stop, and utility methods\n */\nexport function agent(options) {\n  const {\n    tool,\n    workingDirectory,\n    prompt,\n    systemPrompt,\n    model,\n    isolation = 'none',\n    screenName,\n    containerName,\n    json = false,\n    resume,\n    toolOptions = {},\n  } = options;\n\n  // Validate required options\n  if (!tool) {\n    throw new Error('tool is required');\n  }\n  if (!workingDirectory) {\n    throw new Error('workingDirectory is required');\n  }\n  if (isolation === 'screen' && !screenName) {\n    throw new Error('screenName is required for screen isolation');\n  }\n  if (isolation === 'docker' && !containerName) {\n    throw new Error('containerName is required for docker isolation');\n  }\n\n  // Get tool configuration if available\n  const toolConfig = isToolSupported({ toolName: tool })\n    ? getTool({ toolName: tool })\n    : null;\n\n  let processHandle = null;\n  let removeSignalHandler = null;\n  let command = null;\n  let outputStream = null;\n  let sessionId = null;\n\n  /**\n   * Start the agent (non-blocking)\n   * @param {Object} [startOptions] - Start options\n   * @param {boolean} [startOptions.dryRun] - If true, just show the command\n   * @param {boolean} [startOptions.detached] - Run in detached mode\n   * @param {boolean} [startOptions.attached=true] - Stream output to console\n   * @param {Function} [startOptions.onMessage] - Callback for JSON messages (streaming)\n   * @param {Function} [startOptions.onOutput] - Callback for raw output chunks\n   * @returns {Promise<void>} Resolves when process is started (not when it exits)\n   */\n  const start = async (startOptions = {}) => {\n    const {\n      dryRun = false,\n      detached = false,\n      attached = true,\n      onMessage,\n      onOutput,\n    } = startOptions;\n\n    // Create output stream for JSON parsing if in JSON mode or callbacks provided\n    if (json || onMessage) {\n      outputStream = createOutputStream({\n        onMessage: onMessage ? (data) => onMessage(data.message) : undefined,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Build the command with tool-specific options\n    const commandOptions = {\n      tool,\n      workingDirectory,\n      prompt,\n      systemPrompt,\n      isolation,\n      screenName,\n      containerName,\n      detached,\n    };\n\n    // Add tool-specific options if tool is known\n    if (toolConfig) {\n      commandOptions.model = model;\n      commandOptions.json = json;\n      commandOptions.resume = resume;\n      Object.assign(commandOptions, toolOptions);\n    }\n\n    command = buildAgentCommand(commandOptions);\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      console.log('Dry run - command that would be executed:');\n      console.log(command);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Setup signal handler for graceful shutdown\n    if (!detached && isolation === 'none') {\n      removeSignalHandler = setupSignalHandler(async () => {\n        console.log('Propagating shutdown to agent...');\n        // The process will be terminated naturally by SIGINT\n      });\n    }\n\n    if (detached) {\n      // For detached mode, use executeDetached\n      processHandle = await executeDetached(command);\n      console.log(`Agent started in detached mode`);\n      if (isolation === 'screen') {\n        console.log(`Screen session: ${screenName}`);\n      } else if (isolation === 'docker') {\n        console.log(`Container: ${containerName}`);\n      }\n    } else {\n      // For attached mode, start command without waiting\n      const commandOptions = { attached };\n\n      // Add output handling for streaming\n      if (onOutput) {\n        commandOptions.onStdout = (chunk) => {\n          onOutput({ type: 'stdout', data: chunk });\n          if (outputStream) {\n            outputStream.process({ chunk });\n          }\n        };\n        commandOptions.onStderr = (chunk) => {\n          onOutput({ type: 'stderr', data: chunk });\n        };\n      } else if (outputStream) {\n        commandOptions.onStdout = (chunk) => {\n          outputStream.process({ chunk });\n        };\n      }\n\n      processHandle = await startCommand(command, commandOptions);\n    }\n  };\n\n  /**\n   * Stop the agent and collect output\n   * @param {Object} [stopOptions] - Stop options\n   * @param {boolean} [stopOptions.dryRun] - If true, just show the command\n   * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with exitCode, output.plain, output.parsed, and session info\n   */\n  const stop = async (stopOptions = {}) => {\n    const { dryRun = false } = stopOptions;\n\n    // For isolation modes, send stop command\n    if (isolation === 'screen' || isolation === 'docker') {\n      let stopCommand;\n      if (isolation === 'screen') {\n        if (!screenName) {\n          throw new Error('screenName is required to stop screen session');\n        }\n        stopCommand = buildScreenStopCommand(screenName);\n      } else if (isolation === 'docker') {\n        if (!containerName) {\n          throw new Error('containerName is required to stop docker container');\n        }\n        stopCommand = buildDockerStopCommand(containerName);\n      }\n\n      if (dryRun) {\n        console.log('Dry run - command that would be executed:');\n        console.log(stopCommand);\n        return { exitCode: 0, output: { plain: '', parsed: null } };\n      }\n\n      const result = await executeCommand(stopCommand, {\n        dryRun,\n        attached: true,\n      });\n      return {\n        exitCode: result.exitCode,\n        output: {\n          plain: result.stdout,\n          parsed: null, // Stop commands don't produce parsed output\n        },\n      };\n    }\n\n    // For no isolation, wait for process to complete and collect output\n    if (isolation === 'none') {\n      if (!processHandle) {\n        throw new Error('Agent not started or already stopped');\n      }\n\n      // Wait for the process to exit\n      const exitCode = await processHandle.waitForExit();\n      const { stdout, stderr } = processHandle.getOutput();\n\n      // Combine stdout and stderr for plain output\n      const plainOutput = stdout + (stderr ? `\\n${stderr}` : '');\n\n      // Flush output stream if we have one\n      if (outputStream) {\n        outputStream.flush();\n      }\n\n      // Try to parse JSON messages (use output stream messages if available, otherwise parse)\n      let parsedOutput;\n      if (outputStream && outputStream.getMessages().length > 0) {\n        parsedOutput = outputStream.getMessages();\n      } else {\n        parsedOutput = parseJsonMessages({\n          output: plainOutput,\n          toolName: tool,\n        });\n      }\n\n      // Extract session ID if tool supports it\n      if (toolConfig && toolConfig.extractSessionId) {\n        sessionId = toolConfig.extractSessionId({ output: plainOutput });\n      }\n\n      // Extract usage if tool supports it\n      let usage = null;\n      if (toolConfig && toolConfig.extractUsage) {\n        usage = toolConfig.extractUsage({ output: plainOutput });\n      }\n\n      // Clean up signal handler\n      if (removeSignalHandler) {\n        removeSignalHandler();\n        removeSignalHandler = null;\n      }\n\n      return {\n        exitCode,\n        output: {\n          plain: plainOutput,\n          parsed: parsedOutput,\n        },\n        sessionId,\n        usage,\n      };\n    }\n\n    throw new Error(`Unsupported isolation mode: ${isolation}`);\n  };\n\n  /**\n   * Get the current session ID (if available)\n   * @returns {string|null} Session ID or null\n   */\n  const getSessionId = () => sessionId;\n\n  /**\n   * Get all collected messages from the output stream\n   * @returns {Object[]} Array of parsed messages\n   */\n  const getMessages = () => {\n    if (outputStream) {\n      return outputStream.getMessages();\n    }\n    return [];\n  };\n\n  /**\n   * Get tool configuration\n   * @returns {Object|null} Tool configuration or null\n   */\n  const getToolConfig = () => toolConfig;\n\n  return {\n    start,\n    stop,\n    getSessionId,\n    getMessages,\n    getToolConfig,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a new JSON input stream for sending messages to an agent\n * @param {Object} [options] - Options\n * @param {boolean} [options.compact=true] - Use compact JSON\n * @returns {JsonInputStream} Input stream\n */\nexport function createJsonInputStream(options = {}) {\n  return createInputStream(options);\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a new JSON output stream for parsing agent output\n * @param {Object} [options] - Options\n * @param {Function} [options.onMessage] - Callback for each message\n * @param {Function} [options.onError] - Callback for parse errors\n * @returns {JsonOutputStream} Output stream\n */\nexport function createJsonOutputStream(options = {}) {\n  return createOutputStream(options);\n}\n\n// Export other utilities\nexport {\n  buildAgentCommand,\n  buildScreenStopCommand,\n  buildDockerStopCommand,\n} from './command-builder.mjs';\nexport {\n  executeCommand,\n  executeDetached,\n  setupSignalHandler,\n} from         "content": "/**\n * Execute commands using command-stream\n */\n\nimport { getCommandStream } from './utils/loader.mjs';\n\n/**\n * Execute a command and return the result\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {Object} [options] - Execution options\n * @param {boolean} [options.dryRun] - If true, just return the command without executing\n * @param {boolean} [options.attached] - If true, stream output to console\n * @param {Function} [options.onStdout] - Callback for stdout chunks\n * @param {Function} [options.onStderr] - Callback for stderr chunks\n * @param {Function} [options.onExit] - Callback for exit code\n * @returns {Promise<{exitCode: number, stdout: string, stderr: string}>} Execution result\n */\nexport async function executeCommand(command, options = {}) {\n  const {\n    dryRun = false,\n    attached = true,\n    onStdout,\n    onStderr,\n    onExit,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (dryRun) {\n    console.log('Dry run - command that would be executed:');\n    console.log(command);\n    return { exitCode: 0, stdout: '', stderr: '', command };\n  }\n\n  const { $ } = await getCommandStream();\n  const commandStream = $`${command}`;\n\n  let stdout = '';\n  let stderr = '';\n  let exitCode = 0;\n\n  try {\n    for await (const chunk of commandStream.stream()) {\n      if (chunk.type === 'stdout') {\n        stdout += chunk.data;\n        if (attached) {\n          process.stdout.write(chunk.data);\n        }\n        if (onStdout) {\n          onStdout(chunk.data);\n        }\n      } else if (chunk.type === 'stderr') {\n        stderr += chunk.data;\n        if (attached) {\n          process.stderr.write(chunk.data);\n        }\n        if (onStderr) {\n          onStderr(chunk.data);\n        }\n      } else if (chunk.type === 'exit') {\n        exitCode = chunk.code;\n        if (onExit) {\n          onExit(chunk.code);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error('Command execution failed:', error.message);\n    exitCode = 1;\n    stderr += error.message;\n  }\n\n  return { exitCode, stdout, stderr, command };\n}\n\n/**\n * Start a command execution without waiting for completion\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {Object} [options] - Execution options\n * @param {boolean} [options.attached] - If true, stream output to console\n * @returns {Promise<Object>} Process handle with methods to interact with the process\n */\nexport async function startCommand(command, options = {}) {\n  const { attached = true } = options;\n\n  const { $ } = await getCommandStream();\n  const commandStream = $`${command}`;\n\n  let stdout = '';\n  let stderr = '';\n  let exitCode = null;\n  let hasExited = false;\n  let resolveExit;\n\n  const exitPromise = new Promise((resolve) => {\n    resolveExit = resolve;\n  });\n\n  // Start processing the stream in the background\n  (async () => {\n    try {\n      for await (const chunk of commandStream.stream()) {\n        if (chunk.type === 'stdout') {\n          stdout += chunk.data;\n          if (attached) {\n            process.stdout.write(chunk.data);\n          }\n        } else if (chunk.type === 'stderr') {\n          stderr += chunk.data;\n          if (attached) {\n            process.stderr.write(chunk.data);\n          }\n        } else if (chunk.type === 'exit') {\n          exitCode = chunk.code;\n          hasExited = true;\n          resolveExit(chunk.code);\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n      // If we reach here without exit event, resolve with error\n      if (!hasExited) {\n        exitCode = 0;\n        hasExited = true;\n        resolveExit(0);\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error('Command execution failed:', error.message);\n      exitCode = 1;\n      stderr += error.message;\n      hasExited = true;\n      resolveExit(1);\n    }\n  })();\n\n  // Give the stream a moment to start\n  await Promise.resolve();\n\n  return {\n    command,\n    waitForExit: () => exitPromise,\n    getOutput: () => ({ stdout, stderr, exitCode, hasExited }),\n    commandStream,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command in the background (detached)\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @returns {Promise<{pid: number|null}>} Process information\n */\nexport async function executeDetached(command) {\n  const { spawn } = await import('child_process');\n\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    try {\n      const child = spawn('bash', ['-c', command], {\n        detached: true,\n        stdio: 'ignore',\n      });\n\n      child.unref();\n\n      resolve({ pid: child.pid });\n    } catch (error) {\n      reject(error);\n    }\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Setup CTRL+C handler for graceful shutdown\n * @param {Function} cleanupFn - Function to call on CTRL+C\n * @returns {Function} Function to remove the handler\n */\nexport function setupSignalHandler(cleanupFn) {\n  // Detect runtime\n  const isDeno = typeof Deno !== 'undefined';\n  const isNode =\n    typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.versions && process.versions.node;\n\n  if (isDeno) {\n    // Deno signal handling\n    const handler = async () => {\n      console.log('\\nReceived SIGINT, shutting down gracefully...');\n      try {\n        await cleanupFn();\n      } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Error during cleanup:', error.message);\n      }\n      Deno.exit(0);\n    };\n\n    // Listen for SIGINT in Deno\n    Deno.addSignalListener('SIGINT', handler);\n\n    return () => {\n      try {\n        Deno.removeSignalListener('SIGINT', handler);\n      } catch {\n        // Signal listener may already be removed\n      }\n    };\n  } else if (isNode) {\n    // Node.js signal handling\n    const handler = async (signal) => {\n      console.log(`\\nReceived ${signal}, shutting down gracefully...`);\n      try {\n        await cleanupFn();\n      } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Error during cleanup:', error.message);\n      }\n      process.exit(0);\n    };\n\n    process.on('SIGINT', handler);\n    process.on('SIGTERM', handler);\n\n    return () => {\n      process.off('SIGINT', handler);\n      process.off('SIGTERM', handler);\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Return no-op for other runtimes\n  return () => {};\n}",

Options\n * @param {string} [options.prompt] - User prompt\n * @param {string} [options.systemPrompt] - System prompt\n * @param {string} [options.model] - Model to use\n * @param {boolean} [options.print] - Options\n * @param {string} options.workingDirectory - Working directory\n * @param {string} [options.prompt] - User prompt\n * @param {string} [options.systemPrompt] - System prompt\n * @param {string} [options.model] - Model to use\n * @param {boolean} [options.print] - Print mode (non-interactive)\n * @param {boolean} [options.json] - JSON output mode\n * @param {string} [options.resume] - Resume session ID\n * @returns {string} Complete command string\n */\nexport function buildCommand(options) {\n  // eslint-disable-next-line no-unused-vars\n  const { workingDirectory, ...argOptions } = options;\n  const args = buildArgs(argOptions);\n  return `claude ${args.map(escapeArg).join(' ')}`.trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Escape an argument for shell usage\n * @param {string} arg - Argument to escape\n * @returns {string} Escaped argument\n */\nfunction escapeArg(arg) {\n  // If argument contains spaces, quotes, or special chars, wrap in quotes\n  if (/[\"\\s$`\\\\]/.test(arg)) {\n    return `\"${arg.replace(/\"/g, '\\\\\"').replace(/\\$/g, '\\\\$').replace(/`/g, '\\\\`').replace(/\\\\/g, '\\\\\\\\')}\"`;\n  }\n  return arg;\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse JSON messages from Claude output\n * Claude outputs NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) in JSON mode\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.output - Raw output string\n * @returns {Object[]} Array of parsed JSON messages\n */\nexport function parseOutput(options) {\n  const { output } = options;\n  const messages = [];\n  const lines = output.split('\\n');\n\n  for (const line of lines) {\n    const trimmed = line.trim();\n    if (!trimmed || !trimmed.startsWith('{')) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);\n      messages.push(parsed);\n    } catch {\n      // Skip lines that aren't valid JSON\n    }\n  }\n\n  return messages;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract session ID from Claude output\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.output - Raw output string\n * @returns {string|null} Session ID or null\n */\nexport function extractSessionId(options) {\n  const { output } = options;\n  const messages = parseOutput({ output });\n\n  for (const msg of messages) {\n    if (msg.session_id) {\n      return msg.session_id;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract usage statistics from Claude output\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.output - Raw output string\n * @returns {Object} Usage statistics\n */\nexport function extractUsage(options) {\n  const { output } = options;\n  const messages = parseOutput({ output });\n\n  const usage = {\n    inputTokens: 0,\n    outputTokens: 0,\n    cacheCreationTokens: 0,\n    cacheReadTokens: 0,\n  };\n\n  for (const msg of messages) {\n    if (msg.message?.usage) {\n      const u = msg.message.usage;\n      if (u.input_tokens) {\n        usage.inputTokens += u.input_tokens;\n      }\n      if (u.output_tokens) {\n        usage.outputTokens += u.output_tokens;\n      }\n      if (u.cache_creation_input_tokens) {\n        usage.cacheCreationTokens += u.cache_creation_input_tokens;\n      }\n      if (u.cache_read_input_tokens) {\n        usage.cacheReadTokens += u.cache_read_input_tokens;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return usage;\n}\n\n/**\n * Claude tool configuration\n */\nexport const claudeTool = {\n  name: 'claude',\n  displayName: 'Claude Code CLI',\n  executable: 'claude',\n  supportsJsonOutput: true,\n  supportsJsonInput: false, // Claude doesn't support JSON streaming input yet\n  supportsSystemPrompt: true,\n  supportsResume: true,\n  defaultModel: 'sonnet',\n  modelMap,\n  mapModelToId,\n  buildArgs,\n  buildCommand,\n  parseOutput,\n  extractSessionId,\n  extractUsage,\n};",

Options\n * @param {string} options.model - Model alias or full ID\n * @returns {string} Full model ID\n */\nexport function mapModelToId(options) {\n  const { model } = options;\n  return modelMap[model] || model;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build command line arguments for Claude\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} [options.prompt] - User prompt\n * @param {string} [options.systemPrompt] - System prompt\n * @param {string} [options.model] - Model to use\n * @param {boolean} [options.print] - Print mode (non-interactive)\n * @param {boolean} [options.json] - JSON output mode\n * @param {string} [options.resume] - Resume session ID\n * @returns {string[]} Array of CLI arguments\n */\nexport function buildArgs(options) {\n  const {\n    prompt,\n    systemPrompt,\n    model,\n    print = false,\n    json = false,\n    resume,\n  } = options;\n\n  const args = [];\n\n  if (model) {\n    const mappedModel = mapModelToId({ model });\n    args.push('--model', mappedModel);\n  }\n\n  if (prompt) {\n    args.push('--prompt', prompt);\n  }\n\n  if (systemPrompt) {\n    args.push('--system-prompt', systemPrompt);\n  }\n\n  if (print) {\n    args.push('-p'); // Print mode\n  }\n\n  if (json) {\n    args.push('--output-format', 'json');\n  }\n\n  if (resume) {\n    args.push('--resume', resume);\n  }\n\n  return args;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build complete command string for Claude\n * @param {Object} options - Command options\n * @param {string} options.tool - The CLI tool to use (e.g., 'claude', 'codex', 'opencode', 'agent')\n * @param {string} options.workingDirectory - Working directory path\n * @param {string} [options.prompt] - Prompt for the agent\n * @param {string} [options.systemPrompt] - System prompt for the agent\n * @param {string} [options.model] - Model to use (tool-specific)\n * @param {boolean} [options.json] - Enable JSON output mode\n * @param {string} [options.resume] - Resume session ID (tool-specific)\n * @param {string} [options.isolation] - Isolation mode: 'none', 'screen', 'docker'\n * @param {string} [options.screenName] - Screen session name (for screen isolation)\n * @param {string} [options.containerName] - Container name (for docker isolation)\n * @param {boolean} [options.detached] - Run in detached mode\n * @returns {string} The command string\n */\nexport function buildAgentCommand(options) {\n  const {\n    tool,\n    workingDirectory,\n    prompt,\n    systemPrompt,\n    model,\n    json,\n    resume,\n    isolation = 'none',\n    screenName,\n    containerName,\n    detached = false,\n    ...toolOptions\n  } = options;\n\n  // Build base command using tool-specific builder if available\n  let baseCommand;\n\n  if (isToolSupported({ toolName: tool })) {\n    const toolConfig = getTool({ toolName: tool });\n    if (toolConfig.buildCommand) {\n      // Use tool-specific command builder\n      baseCommand = toolConfig.buildCommand({\n        workingDirectory,\n        prompt,\n        systemPrompt,\n        model,\n        json,\n        resume,\n        ...toolOptions,\n      });\n    } else {\n      // Fall back to generic command builder\n      baseCommand = buildToolCommand({\n        tool,\n        workingDirectory,\n        prompt,\n        systemPrompt,\n      });\n    }\n  } else {\n    // Unknown tool, use generic command builder\n    baseCommand = buildToolCommand({\n      tool,\n      workingDirectory,\n      prompt,\n      systemPrompt,\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Wrap in bash -c with working directory change\n  let fullCommand = `bash -c \"cd ${escapeForBashC(workingDirectory)} && ${escapeForBashC(baseCommand)}\"`;\n\n  // Apply isolation wrapper\n  if (isolation === 'screen') {\n    fullCommand = buildScreenCommand({\n      baseCommand: fullCommand,\n      screenName,\n      detached,\n    });\n  } else if (isolation === 'docker') {\n    fullCommand = buildDockerCommand({\n      baseCommand: fullCommand,\n      containerName,\n      workingDirectory,\n      detached,\n    });\n  }\n\n  return fullCommand;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build the base tool command (generic)\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.tool - Tool name\n * @param {string} options.workingDirectory - Working directory\n * @param {string} [options.prompt] - Prompt\n * @param {string} [options.systemPrompt] - System prompt\n * @returns {string} Base command\n */\nfunction buildToolCommand(options) {\n  const { tool, prompt, systemPrompt } = options;\n  let toolCommand = tool;\n\n  if (prompt) {\n    toolCommand += ` --prompt \"${escapeQuotes(prompt)}\"`;\n  }\n\n  if (systemPrompt) {\n    toolCommand += ` --system-prompt \"${escapeQuotes(systemPrompt)}\"`;\n  }\n\n  return toolCommand;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build screen isolation command\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.baseCommand - Base command to wrap\n * @param {string} [options.screenName] - Screen session name\n * @param {boolean} [options.detached] - Detached mode\n * @returns {string} Screen command\n */\nfunction buildScreenCommand(options) {\n  const { baseCommand, screenName, detached = false } = options;\n  const sessionName = screenName || `agent-${Date.now()}`;\n\n  if (detached) {\n    // Start detached screen session\n    return `screen -dmS \"${sessionName}\" bash -c '${escapeQuotes(baseCommand)}'`;\n  } else {\n    // Start attached screen session\n    return `screen -S \"${sessionName}\" bash -c '${escapeQuotes(baseCommand)}'`;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Build docker isolation command\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.baseCommand - Base command to wrap\n * @param {string} [options.containerName] - Container name\n * @param {string} options.workingDirectory - Working directory to mount\n * @param {boolean} [options.detached] - Detached mode\n * @returns {string} Docker command\n */\nfunction buildDockerCommand(options) {\n  const {\n    baseCommand,\n    containerName,\n    workingDirectory,\n    detached = false,\n  } = options;\n  const name = containerName || `agent-${Date.now()}`;\n\n  let dockerCommand = 'docker run';\n\n  if (detached) {\n    dockerCommand += ' -d';\n  } else {\n    dockerCommand += ' -it';\n  }\n\n  dockerCommand += ` --name \"${name}\"`;\n  dockerCommand += ` -v \"${workingDirectory}:${workingDirectory}\"`;\n  dockerCommand += ` -w \"${workingDirectory}\"`;\n  dockerCommand += ` node:18-slim`;\n  dockerCommand += ` bash -c '${escapeQuotes(baseCommand)}'`;\n\n  return dockerCommand;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build stop command for screen sessions\n * @param {string} screenName - Screen session name\n * @returns {string} Stop command\n */\nexport function buildScreenStopCommand(screenName) {\n  return `screen -S \"${screenName}\" -X quit`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build stop command for docker containers\n * @param {string} containerName - Container name\n * @returns {string} Stop command\n */\nexport function buildDockerStopCommand(containerName) {\n  return `docker stop \"${containerName}\" && docker rm \"${containerName}\"`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Escape quotes in strings for shell commands (single quotes)\n * @param {string} str - String to escape\n * @returns {string} Escaped string\n */\nfunction escapeQuotes(str) {\n  if (!str) {\n    return '';\n  }\n  return str.replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Escape strings for use inside bash -c \"...\"\n * @param {string} str - String to escape\n * @returns {string} Escaped string\n */\nfunction escapeForBashC(str) {\n  if (!str) {\n    return '';\n  }\n  // Escape backslashes first, then double quotes, dollar signs, and backticks\n  return str\n    .replace(/\\\\/g, '\\\\\\\\')\n    .replace(/\"/g, '\\\\\"')\n    .replace(/\\$/g, '\\\\$')\n    .replace(/`/g, '\\\\`');\n}\n\n/**\n * Build stdin piping command for tools that accept input via stdin\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {string} options.input - Input to pipe\n * @param {string} options.command - Command to pipe to\n * @returns {string} Piped command\n */\nexport function buildPipedCommand(options) {\n  const { input, command } = options;\n  const escapedInput = (input || '').replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\");\n  return `printf '%s' '${escapedInput}' | ${command}`;\n}",

command line arguments - now needs yargs and hideBin passed in\nexport const parseArguments = async (yargs, hideBin) => {\n  const rawArgs = hideBin(process.argv);\n  // Use .parse() instead of .argv to ensure .strict() mode works correctly\n  // When you call yargs(args) and use .argv, strict mode doesn't trigger\n  // See: https://github.com/yargs/yargs/issues - .strict() only works with .parse()\n\n  let argv;\n  try {\n    // Suppress stderr output from yargs during parsing to prevent validation errors from appearing\n    // This prevents \"YError: Not enough arguments\" from polluting stderr (issue #583)\n    // Save the original stderr.write\n    const originalStderrWrite = process.stderr.write;\n    const stderrBuffer = [];\n\n    // Temporarily override stderr.write to capture output\n    process.stderr.write = function (chunk, encoding, callback) {\n      stderrBuffer.push(chunk.toString());\n      // Call the callback if provided (for compatibility)\n      if (typeof encoding === 'function') {\n        encoding();\n      } else if (typeof callback === 'function') {\n        callback();\n      }\n      return true;\n    };\n\n    try {\n      argv = await createYargsConfig(yargs()).parse(rawArgs);\n    } finally {\n      // Always restore stderr.write\n      process.stderr.write = originalStderrWrite;\n\n      // In verbose mode, show what was captured from stderr (for debugging)\n      if (global.verboseMode && stderrBuffer.length > 0) {\n        const captured = stderrBuffer.join('');\n        if (captured.trim()) {\n          console.error('[Suppressed yargs stderr]:', captured);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    // Yargs throws errors for validation issues\n    // If the error is about unknown arguments (strict mode), re-throw it\n    if (error.message && error.message.includes('Unknown arguments')) {\n      throw error;\n    }\n    // For other validation errors, show a warning in verbose mode\n    if (error.message && global.verboseMode) {\n      console.error('Yargs parsing warning:', error.message);\n    }\n    // Try to get the argv even with the error\n    argv = error.argv || {};\n  }\n\n  // Post-processing: Fix model default for opencode and codex tools\n  // Yargs doesn't properly handle dynamic defaults based on other arguments,\n  // so we need to handle this manually after parsing\n  const model*************vided = rawArgs.includes('--model') || rawArgs.includes('-m');\n\n  // Normalize alias flags: legacy --skip-tool-check and --skip-claude-check behave like --skip-tool-connection-check\n  if (argv) {\n    // Support deprecated flags\n    if (argv.skipToolCheck || argv.skipClaudeCheck) {\n      argv.skipToolConnectionCheck = true;\n    }\n    // Support negated deprecated flag: --no-tool-check becomes --no-tool-connection-check\n    if (argv.toolCheck === false) {\n      argv.toolConnectionCheck = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (argv.tool === 'opencode' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for opencode\n    argv.model = 'grok-code-fast-1';\n  } else if (argv.tool === 'codex' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for codex\n    argv.model = 'gpt-5';\n  } else if (argv.tool === 'agent' && !modelExplicitlyProvided) {\n    // User did not explicitly provide --model, so use the correct default for agent\n    argv.model = 'grok-code';\n  }\n\n  // Validate mutual exclusivity of --claude-file and --gitkeep-file\n  // Check if both are explicitly enabled (user passed both --claude-file and --gitkeep-file)\n  if (argv.claudeFile && argv.gitkeepFile) {\n    // Check if they were explicitly set via command line\n    const claudeFileExplicit = rawArgs.includes('--claude-file');\n    const gitkeepFileExplicit = rawArgs.includes('--gitkeep-file');\n\n    if (claudeFileExplicit && gitkeepFileExplicit) {\n      throw new Error('--claude-file and --gitkeep-file are mutually exclusive. Please use only one.');\n    }\n\n    // If only one is explicit, turn off the other\n    if (gitkeepFileExplicit && !claudeFileExplicit) {\n      argv.claudeFile = false;\n    } else if (claudeFileExplicit && !gitkeepFileExplicit) {\n      argv.gitkeepFile = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check for both being disabled (both --no-claude-file and --no-gitkeep-file)\n  const noClaudeFile = rawArgs.includes('--no-claude-file');\n  const noGitkeepFile = rawArgs.includes('--no-gitkeep-file');\n\n  if (noClaudeFile && noGitkeepFile) {\n    throw new Error('Cannot disable both --claude-file and --gitkeep-file. At least one must be enabled for PR creation.');\n  }\n\n  // If user explicitly set --no-claude-file, enable gitkeep-file\n  if (noClaudeFile && !argv.gitkeepFile) {\n    argv.gitkeepFile = true;\n    argv.claudeFile = false;\n  }\n\n  // If user explicitly set --no-gitkeep-file, enable claude-file (this is the default anyway)\n  if (noGitkeepFile && !argv.claudeFile) {\n    argv.claudeFile = true;\n    argv.gitkeepFile = false;\n  }\n\n  return argv;\n};\n",

} = params;\n\n  const tool = argv.tool || 'claude';\n\n  try {\n    const { agent } = await getAgentCommander();\n\n    // Import prompt building functions based on tool\n    let buildUserPrompt, buildSystemPrompt = agentLib.buildSystemPrompt;\n    } else {\n      throw new Error(`Unknown tool: ${tool}`);\n    }\n\n    // Build prompts using existing prompt building logic\n    const prompt = similar to link an issue', { level: 'warning' });\n      // Set issueNumber to PR number as fallback\n      issueNumber = prNumber;\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    reportError(error, {\n      context: 'pr_processing',\n      prNumber,\n      operation: 'process_pull_request',\n    });\n    await log(`Error: Failed to process PR: ${cleanErrorMessage(error)}`, { level: 'error' });\n    await safeExit(1, 'Failed to process PR');\n  }\n} else {\n  // Traditional issue mode\n  issueNumber = urlNumber;\n  await log(`📝 Issue mode: Working with issue #${issueNumber}`);\n}\n// Create or find $,\n  });\n\n  // Verify default branch and status using the new module\n  const defaultBranch = await verifyDefaultBranchAndStatus({\n    tempDir,\n    log,\n    formatAligned,\n    $,\n  });\n  //    await log('     PR creation may have failed without throwing an error.');\n    }\n    await log('');\n    await log('  💡 Why this is critical:');\n    await log('     The solve command requires a await log('');\n    await log('  Option 2: Start fresh without continue mode');\n    await log(`     ./solve.mjs \"${issueUrl}\" --auto-pull-request-creation`);\n    await log('');\n    await log('  Option 3: Disable auto-PR creation (Claude will create it)');\n    await log(`     ./solve.mjs \"${issueUrl}\" --no-auto-pull-request-creation`);\n    await log('');\n    await safeExit(1, 'No PR available');\n  }\n\n  if (isContinueMode) {\n    await log(`\\n${formatAligned('🔄', 'Continue mode:', 'ACTIVE')}`);\n    await log(formatAligned('', 'Using existing PR:', `#${prNumber}`, 2));\n    await log(formatAligned('', 'PR URL:', prUrl, 2));\n  } else if (!argv.autoPullRequestCreation) {\n    await log(`\\n${formatAligned('⏭️', 'Auto PR creation:', 'DISABLED')}`);\n    await });\n\n  // Prepare feedback and timestamps using the new module\n  const { feedbackLines: preparedFeedbackLines, referenceTime } = await prepareFeedbackAndTimestamps({\n    prNumber,\n   const opencodeLib = await import('./opencode.lib.mjs');\n    const { executeOpenCode } = opencodeLib;\n    const opencodePath = process.env.OPENCODE_PATH || 'opencode';\n\n    toolResult = await      const logUploadSuccess = await attachLogToGitHub({\n            logFile: getLogFile(),\n            targetType: 'pr',\n            targetNumber: prNumber,\n            owner,\n            repo,\n            $,\n            log,\n            sanitizeLogContent,\n            // Mark this as a usage limit case for proper formatting\n            isUsageLimit: true,\n            limitResetTime: 'OpenCode' : (argv.tool || 'AI tool').toString().toLowerCase() === 'agent' ? Auto-continue is enabled with \\`--auto-continue-on-limit-reset\\`.\\n\\n**Reset time:** ${global.limitResetTime}\\n**Wait time:** ${formatWaitTime(waitMs)} (days:hours:minutes:seconds)\\n\\nThe session will automatically resume when the limit resets.\\n\\nSession ID: \\`${sessionId}\\`${resumeCmd ? `\\n\\nTo resume manually:\\n\\`\\`\\`bash\\n${resumeCmd}\\n\\`\\`\\`` : ''}`;\n\n            const commentResult = await $`gh pr comment ${prNumber} --repo ${owner}/${repo} --body ${waitingComment}`;\n            if (commentResult.code === 0) {\n              await log('   Posted waiting comment to PR');\n            }\n          } catch (error) {\n            await log(`   Warning: Could not post waiting comment: ${cleanErrorMessage(error)}`, { verbose: true });\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (!success) {\n    // Show claude resume command only for --tool claude (or default) on failure\n    // Uses the (cd ... && claude --resume ...) pattern for a fully copyable, executable command\n    const toolForFailure = argv.tool || 'claude';\n    if (sessionId && toolForFailure === 'claude') {\n      const claudeResumeCmd = buildClaudeResumeCommand({ tempDir, sessionId, model: argv.model });\n      await log('');\n      await log('💡 To continue this session in Claude Code interactive mode:');\n      await log('');\n      await log(`   ${claudeResumeCmd}`);\n      await log('');\n    }\n\n    // If --attach-logs is enabled and we have a PR, attach failure logs before exiting\n    if (shouldAttachLogs && sessionId && global.createdPR && global.createdPR.number) {\n      await log('\\n📄 Attaching failure logs to Pull Request...');\n      try {\n        // Build Claude CLI resume command\n        const tool = argv.tool || 'claude';\n        const resumeCommand = sessionId && tool === 'claude' ? 'OpenCode' : (argv.tool || 'AI tool').toString().toLowerCase() === 'agent' ? argv['autoRestartOnUncommittedChanges'] !== false;\n  const shouldRestart = await checkForUncommittedChanges(tempDir, owner, repo, branchName, $, log, shouldAutoCommit, autoRestartEnabled);\n\n  // file\n  await showSessionSummary(sessionId, limitReached, argv, issueUrl, tempDir, shouldAttachLogs);\n\n  // Search for newly created pull requests and comments\n  // Pass shouldRestart to prevent uncommitted changes\n  if (argv.verbose) {\n    await log('');\n    await log('🔍 Auto-restart debug:', { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   argv.watch (user flag): ${argv.watch}`, { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   prNumber: ${prNumber || 'null'}`, { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   prBranch: ${prBranch || 'null'}`, { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   branchName: ${branchName}`, { verbose: true });\n    await log(`   isContinueMode: ${isContinueMode}`, { verbose: true });\n  }\n\n  // If uncommitted changes detected and auto-commit is disabled, enter temporary watch mode\n  const temporaryWatchMode = shouldRestart && !argv.watch;\n  if (temporaryWatchMode) {\n    await log('');\n    await log('🔄 AUTO-RESTART: Uncommitted changes detected');\n    await log('   Starting 'warning' });\n          await requestMaintainerAccess(owner, repo, prNumber);\n          await log('   Comment posted. Proceeding with own fork instead.', { level: 'warning' });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    await log(`📝 PR branch: ${prBranch}`);\n    // Extract issue number from PR body using GitHub linking extractLinkedIssueNumber(prBody);\n    if (extractedIssueNumber) {\n      issueNumber = extractedIssueNumber;\n      await log(`🔗 Found linked issue #${issueNumber}`);\n    } else {\n      // If no similar to link an issue', { level: 'warning' });\n      // Set issueNumber to PR number as fallback\n      issueNumber = prNumber;\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    reportError(error, {\n      context: 'pr_processing',\n      prNumber,\n      operation: 'process_pull_request',\n    });\n    await log(`Error: Failed to process PR: ${cleanErrorMessage(error)}`, { level: 'error' });\n    await safeExit(1, 'Failed to process PR');\n  }\n} else {\n  // Traditional issue mode\n  issueNumber = urlNumber;\n  await log(`📝 Issue mode: Working with issue #${issueNumber}`);\n}\n// Create or find $,\n  });\n\n  // Verify default branch and status using the new module\n  const defaultBranch = await verifyDefaultBranchAndStatus({\n    tempDir,\n    log,\n    formatAligned,\n    $,\n  });\n  // mergeResult = await $({ cwd: tempDir })`git merge ${defaultBranch} --no-edit`;\n      if (mergeResult.code === 0) {\n        await log(`${formatAligned('✅', 'Merge successful:', 'Pushing merged FAILED:');\n        autoMergeFeedbackLines.push(`git merge ${defaultBranch} was executed but resulted in conflicts that should be resolved first.`);\n        autoMergeFeedbackLines.push('Please ${defaultBranch} failed with error: ${mergeError.message}`);\n      autoMergeFeedbackLines.push('Please check the repository state and resolve any issues.');\n      autoMergeFeedbackLines.push('');\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Initialize PR variables early\n  let prUrl = null;\n\n  // In continue mode, we already have the PR details\n  if (isContinueMode) {\n    prUrl = issueUrl; // The input URL is the PR URL\n    // prNumber is already set from earlier when we parsed the PR\n  }\n\n  // Don't build the prompt yet - we'll build it after we have all the information\n  // This includes PR URL (if await log('');\n    await log('  Option 2: Start fresh without continue mode');\n    await log(`     ./solve.mjs \"${issueUrl}\" --auto-pull-request-creation`);\n    await log('');\n    await log('      const logUploadSuccess = await attachLogToGitHub({\n            logFile: getLogFile(),\n            targetType: 'pr',\n            targetNumber: prNumber,\n            owner,\n            repo,\n 'OpenCode' : (argv.tool || 'AI tool').toString().toLowerCase() === 'agent' ? Auto-continue is enabled with \\`--auto-continue-on-limit-reset\\`.\\n\\n**Reset time:** ${global.limitResetTime}\\n**Wait time:** ${formatWaitTime(waitMs)} (days:hours:minutes:seconds)\\n\\nThe session will automatically resume when the limit resets.\\n\\nSession ID: \\`${sessionId}\\`${resumeCmd ? `\\n\\nTo resume manually:\\n\\`\\`\\`bash\\n${resumeCmd}\\n\\`\\`\\`` : ''}`;\n\n            const commentResult = await $`gh pr comment ${prNumber} --repo ${owner}/${repo} --body ${waitingComment}`;\n            if (commentResult.code === 0) {\n              await log('   Posted waiting comment to PR');\n            }\n          } catch (error) {\n            await log(`   Warning: Could not post waiting comment: ${cleanErrorMessage(error)}`, { verbose: true });\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (!success) {\n    // Show claude resume command only for --tool claude (or default) on failure\n    // Uses the (cd ... && claude --resume ...) pattern for a fully copyable, executable command\n    const toolForFailure = argv.tool || 'claude';\n    if (sessionId && toolForFailure === 'claude') {\n      const claudeResumeCmd = buildClaudeResumeCommand({ tempDir, sessionId, model: argv.model });\n      await log('');\n      await log('💡 To continue this session in Claude Code interactive mode:');\n      await log('');\n      await log(`   ${claudeResumeCmd}`);\n      await log('');\n    }\n\n    // If --attach-logs is enabled and we have a PR, attach failure logs before exiting\n    if (shouldAttachLogs && sessionId && global.createdPR && global.createdPR.number) {\n      await log('\\n📄 Attaching failure logs to Pull Request...');\n      try {\n        // Build Claude CLI resume command\n        const tool = argv.tool || 'claude';\n        const resumeCommand = sessionId && tool === 'claude' ? 'OpenCode' : (argv.tool || 'AI tool').toString().toLowerCase() === 'agent' ? argv['autoRestartOnUncommittedChanges'] !== false;\n  const shouldRestart = await checkForUncommittedChanges(tempDir, owner, repo, branchName, $, log, shouldAutoCommit, autoRestartEnabled);\n\n  // } = await import('child_process');\n      const { promisify } = await import('util');\n      const execAsync = promisify(exec);\n      try {\n        await log(`   🔄 Using repository-by-repository fallback for ${scope}: ${owner}`);\n        // Strategy 1: Try GraphQL approach first (faster but has limitations)\n        // Only try GraphQL for \"all issues\" mode, not for labeled issues\n // First, get list of ALL repositories using gh api with --paginate for unlimited pagination\n        // This approach uses the GitHub API directly to fetch all repositories without any limits\n        // Include isArchived field to filter out archived repositories\n        let repoListCmd;\n        if (scope === 'organization') {\n          repoListCmd = `gh api orgs/${owner}/repos --paginate --jq '.[] | {name: .name, owner: .owner.login, isArchived: .archived}'`;\n        } else {\n          repoListCmd = `gh api users/${owner}/repos --paginate --jq '.[] | {name: .name, owner: .owner.login, execAsync(repoListCmd, { encoding: 'utf8', env: process.env });\n        // Parse the output line by line, as gh api with --jq outputs one JSON object per line\n        const repoLines = repoOutput\n          .trim()\n          .split('\\n')\n          .filter(line => line.trim());\n        const allRepositories = repoLines.map(line => JSON.parse(line));\n        await log(`   📊 Found ${allRepositories.length} repositories`);\n\n        // Filter repositories to only include those owned by the target user/org\n        const ownedRepositories = allRepositories.filter(repo => {\n     const repositories = ownedRepositories.filter(repo => !repo.isArchived);\n        const archivedCount = ownedRepositories.length - repositories.length;\n        if (archivedCount > 0) {\n       const repoName = repo.name;\n            const ownerName = repo.owner?.login || owner;\n            await log(`   🔍 Fetching issues from ${ownerName}/${repoName}...`, { verbose: true });\n\n url,title,number,createdAt`;\n            }\n            // Add delay between repository requests\n            await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000));\n            const repoIssues = await fetchAllIssuesWithPagination(issueCmd);\n            // Add repository information to each issue\n            const issuesWithRepo = repoIssues.map(issue => ({\n              ...issue,\n              repository: {\n                name: repoName,\n                owner: { login: ownerName },\n              },\n            }));\n            collectedIssues.push(...issuesWithRepo);\n            processedRepos++;\n            if (issuesWithRepo.length > 0) {\n              await log(`   ✅ Found ${issuesWithRepo.length} issues in ${ownerName}/${repoName}`, { verbose: true });\n            }\n          } catch (repoError) {\n            reportError(repoError, {\n              context: 'fetchIssuesFromRepositories',\n              repo: repo.name,\n              operation: 'fetch_repo_issues',\n            });\n            await log(`   ⚠️  Failed to fetch issues from ${repo.name}: ${cleanErrorMessage(repoError)}`, {\n              verbose: true,\n            });\n            // Continue with other repositories\n          }\n        }\n\n        await log(`   ✅ Repository fallback complete: ${collectedIssues.length} issues from ${processedRepos}/${repositories.length} repositories`);\n        return collectedIssues;\n      } catch (error) {\n        reportError(error, {\n          context: 'fetchIssuesFromRepositories',\n          owner,\n          scope,\n          operation: 'repository_fallback',\n        });\n        await log(`   ❌ Repository fallback failed: ${cleanErrorMessage(error)}`, { level: 'error' });\n
